page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance belonging to the c2d server family. It is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC 7B13 cores using x86_64 architecture with multithreading enabled. The instance features 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The underlying AMD processor includes a 32 MB L3 cache, 0.5 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, alongside hardware support for AVX, AES, VAES, SHA_NI, and BMI2 instructions.

Performance benchmarks reveal a contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng testing, placing in the top 25%. However, its multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench falls into the average and weak categories respectively, reflecting the physical core count constraint. Memory performance is mixed; while large-block read and write bandwidth tests show average throughput, cached small-block read bandwidth is weak. Similarly, database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput are positioned in the bottom 25% of benchmarks.

The qualitative cost profile of the c2d-highmem-4 reflects its positioning as a resource-dense instance for memory-bound workloads that do not require high parallel compute scaling. The lack of local NVMe storage and weak multi-threaded decompression and compilation performance represent clear tradeoffs. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications with moderate CPU demands, such as caching layers, small-scale database operations, text processing, and lightweight single-threaded tasks.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 32.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage or GPU resources. Benchmark data shows strong single-core CPU performance, though multi-core scaling and cached memory bandwidth are relatively weak. Large-block memory transfers and basic cryptographic operations perform at average levels. Given its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core and moderate multi-threaded performance, this instance is best suited for memory-bound workloads with low-to-moderate parallel processing needs, such as in-memory caching, text processing, and lightweight database tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core performance for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highmem-4 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and no local storage.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for memory-bound workloads.

Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators.
Average performance in large-block memory bandwidth and text processing.
Best suited for memory-intensive workloads with moderate parallel compute needs.
